next_suggestion: 派生 H01 fix: 简化 autonomous-loop.sh L99-179 dead model-fallback 分支为单次 run_round $PRIMARY_MODEL。route-fix，复用 opt-engine-core worktree。

audit_findings.finding: Model fallback logic is dead code (PRIMARY=FALLBACK=qwen3.7-max, threshold=9999) but still executes ~30 lines of branch logic per round. Worse: L142 subshell wraps entire loop body so GLM_FAIL_STREAK/STICKY_FALLBACK/PROBE_COUNTDOWN modifications never propagate to parent shell — sticky fallback would silently fail even if dual-model were restored.
audit_findings.remediation: Option A (recommended): simplify to single run_round $PRIMARY_MODEL, delete sticky/probe branches, keep git history comment. Option B: externalize state to .claude/.model-state.json if dual-model restoration planned.

audit_findings.finding: pgrep -f 'claude.*${PROJECT_DIR}' matches any process whose command line contains the path string (grep itself, vim editing files under that path, other tools passing path as arg). Behavior varies across GNU procps / BusyBox / macOS. False positive → CLAUDE_PROCS > 0 → RESUME_FLAG not set → crashed claude never restarted.
audit_findings.remediation: Use cwd anchoring: iterate pgrep -f '^claude ' pids, check readlink /proc/$pid/cwd equals PROJECT_DIR. Or have autonomous-loop.sh write PID to .claude/.loop.pid and watchdog checks that PID's existence + cwd.

audit_findings.finding: SUCCESS_OUTCOMES={'success','partial_success'} and FAILURE_OUTCOMES={'failure','user_rejected','aborted'} do not include case schema enum values (succeeded/failed/rolled_back/superseded). New cases writing 'succeeded' fall through to inferred branch → accuracy_basis always indeterminate → pattern accuracy computation broken for all post-schema-change cases.
audit_findings.remediation: Add canonicalization map _CANONICAL={'succeeded':'success','failed':'failure','rolled_back':'failure','superseded':'indeterminate'} and apply before OUTCOME_ENUM check. Or expand SUCCESS/FAILURE sets to include both old and new enums.

audit_findings.finding: Bare 'except Exception: continue' swallows MemoryError/KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit. distill-patterns.py already fixed this pattern (SCRIPTS-L04 at L38-46) but index-cases.py was missed. OOM or Ctrl-C during index build silently produces incomplete results.
audit_findings.remediation: Narrow to (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, UnicodeDecodeError) matching distill-patterns.py pattern.

audit_findings.finding: TASK_ID parsed via word-split on python output: 'for TASK_ID in $(echo ... | python3 ...)'. Task IDs containing spaces, newlines, or glob chars (*,?,[) cause incorrect splitting, filename expansion, or illegal branch names. parallel-plan.json is LLM-generated with no ID format enforcement.
audit_findings.remediation: Python outputs NUL-delimited (print(t,end='\0')) + bash 'while IFS= read -r -d "" TASK_ID'. Or validate ID against [a-zA-Z0-9_-] regex in python, exit 1 on violation.

audit_findings.finding: --json flag has action='store_true' + default=True, making the flag permanently True and meaningless. All callers expect JSON so functionally harmless but API is deceptive.
audit_findings.remediation: Remove default=True (keep flag functional) or remove --json entirely (JSON as sole output format).

audit_findings.finding: Bare 'except Exception: return None' same pattern as M02. Should narrow to (OSError, ValueError, TypeError, UnicodeDecodeError) for consistency with SCRIPTS-L04 discipline.
